The {{nihongo|'''Nozu Kofun'''|野津古墳群| Nozu Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Ryūhoku, Kumamoto|Ryūhoku]] neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Hikawa, Kumamoto|Hikawa]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173945 |title= 野津古墳群 |trans-title= Nozu kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> Furthermore, the [[Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]], the largest ''kofun'' in Kumamoto Prefecture is located on the same hill to the north.
==Overview== The Nozu Kofun cluster is located on a hill at an elevation of 90 to 100 meters overlooking the plains of the Hikawa River basin and the [[:en:Yatsushiro Sea|Yatsushiro Sea]] on the western foot of the [[:en:Kyushu Mountains|Kyushu Mountains]] in central Kumamoto Prefecture. It is composed of five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, of which four are currently extant. Several boat-shaped and box-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]] have also been found in the surrounding area.Excavations have been carried out since 1993.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
===Monomi Yagura Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Monomi Yagura Kofun'''|物見櫓古墳||}} is the westernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the first built. It has a length of 62 meters, making it also the smallest in the group. The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southwest. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', or of a surrounding moat. The multi-chamber horizontal-entry [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has a length of 11 meters. The Monomi-Yagura Tomb is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century. Despite having been looted in antiquity, surviving [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include iron arrowheads and iron spears, fragments of armor, glass beads, earrings with pendants, as well as ceramic pottery and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. Among these items the gold earrings with pendants and the ceramic pottery are noteworthy as indications of exchanges with the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], and have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Hikawa Town.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hime-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hime-no-jō Kofun'''|姫ノ城古墳||}} is the northernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the second built. It has a length of 86 meters, and is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 115 meters. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa''. In addition, stone ornaments (two quivers, six lid caps, three lid supports, seven stone shields, etc.) have been found on the outside of the mound, which is the second largest concentration in any ''kofun'' after the [[Yame Kofun Cluster|Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. These items have been designated Important Cultural Properties of Kumamoto Prefecture. Details of the burial chamber are not clear, as it has not been excavated, but [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] surveys indicate a horizontal-entry stone chamber. It is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Naka-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Naka-no-jō Kofun'''|中ノ城古墳||}} is the central tumulus in the group, and is believed to have been the third built. It has a length of 102 meterss, making it the largest in the group. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'', as well as stone ornaments. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 117 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, and was robbed in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. Surviving grave goods found include armor, iron swords, horse equipment, and glass beads, which date the construction to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. The excavated items have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Hikawa Town, and some of these items are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The tumulus was used as a [[:en:Air raid shelter|Air raid shelter]] in World War II.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hashi-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hashi-no-jō Kofun'''|端ノ城古墳||}} is the southernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the last built. It has a length of 68 meters, or a total length overall length including the surrounding moat of about 800 meters The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found on the out of the mound. The burial facility is a horizontal entry stone chamber containing a stone sarcophagus. Despite having been looted, surviving grave goods include iron arrowheads, armor, Chinese scepters, horse fittings, glass ''[[magatama]]'', jasper tubular beads, and Sue pottery pottery. From these items, the tumulus estimated to have been built in the early to mid-6th century during the late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is composed of five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, of which four are currently extant. Several boat-shaped and box-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]] have also been found in the surrounding area.Excavations have been carried out since 1993.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
===Monomi Yagura Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Monomi Yagura Kofun'''|物見櫓古墳||}} is the westernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the first built. It has a length of 62 meters, making it also the smallest in the group. The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southwest. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', or of a surrounding moat. The multi-chamber horizontal-entry [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has a length of 11 meters. The Monomi-Yagura Tomb is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century. Despite having been looted in antiquity, surviving [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include iron arrowheads and iron spears, fragments of armor, glass beads, earrings with pendants, as well as ceramic pottery and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. Among these items the gold earrings with pendants and the ceramic pottery are noteworthy as indications of exchanges with the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], and have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Hikawa Town.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hime-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hime-no-jō Kofun'''|姫ノ城古墳||}} is the northernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the second built. It has a length of 86 meters, and is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 115 meters. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa''. In addition, stone ornaments (two quivers, six lid caps, three lid supports, seven stone shields, etc.) have been found on the outside of the mound, which is the second largest concentration in any ''kofun'' after the [[Yame Kofun Cluster|Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. These items have been designated Important Cultural Properties of Kumamoto Prefecture. Details of the burial chamber are not clear, as it has not been excavated, but [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] surveys indicate a horizontal-entry stone chamber. It is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Naka-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Naka-no-jō Kofun'''|中ノ城古墳||}} is the central tumulus in the group, and is believed to have been the third built. It has a length of 102 meterss, making it the largest in the group. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'', as well as stone ornaments. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 117 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, and was robbed in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. Surviving grave goods found include armor, iron swords, horse equipment, and glass beads, which date the construction to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. The excavated items have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Hikawa Town, and some of these items are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The tumulus was used as a [[:en:Air raid shelter|Air raid shelter]] in World War II.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hashi-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hashi-no-jō Kofun'''|端ノ城古墳||}} is the southernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the last built. It has a length of 68 meters, or a total length overall length including the surrounding moat of about 800 meters The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found on the out of the mound. The burial facility is a horizontal entry stone chamber containing a stone sarcophagus. Despite having been looted, surviving grave goods include iron arrowheads, armor, Chinese scepters, horse fittings, glass ''[[magatama]]'', jasper tubular beads, and Sue pottery pottery. From these items, the tumulus estimated to have been built in the early to mid-6th century during the late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
=== Amazutsu Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Amazutsu Kofun'''|天堤古墳||}} was the fifth tumulus in the cluster. It was bulldozed in 1962 to create an orchard. Stone ornaments were recovered from the site.
<gallery> Monomiyagura Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Monomiyagura Kofun Nakanojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Naka-no-jō Kofun Hashinojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Hashi-no-jō Kofun </gallery>
The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Arisa Station|Arisa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]]

=== Amazutsu Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Amazutsu Kofun'''|天堤古墳||}} was the fifth tumulus in the cluster. It was bulldozed in 1962 to create an orchard. Stone ornaments were recovered from the site.
<gallery> Monomiyagura Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Monomiyagura Kofun Nakanojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Naka-no-jō Kofun Hashinojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Hashi-no-jō Kofun </gallery>
The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of {{ill|Arisa Station|en|Arisa Station|WD=Q4790865|lt=Arisa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|WD=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nozu Kofun Group]][[en:Nozu Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nozu Kofun Group]][[en:Nozu Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nozu Kofun Group]][[en:Nozu Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野津古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55525131]] Wikidata name: Nozu Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nozu Kofun cluster | native_name = 野津古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Himenojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Hime-no-jō Kofun | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nozu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|33|37.77|N|130|41|35.41|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.56049167|N|130.69316667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hikawa, Kumamoto|en|Hikawa, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184800|lt=Hikawa, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|33|37.77|N|130|41|35.41|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Nozu Kofun'''|野津古墳群| Nozu Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Ryūhoku, Kumamoto|Ryūhoku]] neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Hikawa, Kumamoto|en|Hikawa, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184800|lt=Hikawa}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173945 |title= 野津古墳群 |trans-title= Nozu kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> Furthermore, the [[Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]], the largest ''kofun'' in Kumamoto Prefecture is located on the same hill to the north.
==Overview== The Nozu Kofun cluster is located on a hill at an elevation of 90 to 100 meters overlooking the plains of the Hikawa River basin and the {{ill|Yatsushiro Sea|en|Yatsushiro Sea|WD=Q49560|lt=Yatsushiro Sea}} on the western foot of the {{ill|Kyushu Mountains|en|Kyushu Mountains|WD=Q11370238|lt=Kyushu Mountains}} in central Kumamoto Prefecture. It is composed of five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, of which four are currently extant. Several boat-shaped and box-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi}} have also been found in the surrounding area.Excavations have been carried out since 1993.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
===Monomi Yagura Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Monomi Yagura Kofun'''|物見櫓古墳||}} is the westernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the first built. It has a length of 62 meters, making it also the smallest in the group. The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southwest. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but there is no trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', or of a surrounding moat. The multi-chamber horizontal-entry [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has a length of 11 meters. The Monomi-Yagura Tomb is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century. Despite having been looted in antiquity, surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include iron arrowheads and iron spears, fragments of armor, glass beads, earrings with pendants, as well as ceramic pottery and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. Among these items the gold earrings with pendants and the ceramic pottery are noteworthy as indications of exchanges with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}, and have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Hikawa Town.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hime-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hime-no-jō Kofun'''|姫ノ城古墳||}} is the northernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the second built. It has a length of 86 meters, and is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 115 meters. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa''. In addition, stone ornaments (two quivers, six lid caps, three lid supports, seven stone shields, etc.) have been found on the outside of the mound, which is the second largest concentration in any ''kofun'' after the [[Yame Kofun Cluster|Iwatoyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. These items have been designated Important Cultural Properties of Kumamoto Prefecture. Details of the burial chamber are not clear, as it has not been excavated, but {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar}} surveys indicate a horizontal-entry stone chamber. It is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Naka-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Naka-no-jō Kofun'''|中ノ城古墳||}} is the central tumulus in the group, and is believed to have been the third built. It has a length of 102 meterss, making it the largest in the group. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'', as well as stone ornaments. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 117 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, and was robbed in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. Surviving grave goods found include armor, iron swords, horse equipment, and glass beads, which date the construction to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. The excavated items have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Hikawa Town, and some of these items are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. The tumulus was used as a {{ill|Air raid shelter|en|Air raid shelter|WD=Q389959|lt=Air raid shelter}} in World War II.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hashi-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hashi-no-jō Kofun'''|端ノ城古墳||}} is the southernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the last built. It has a length of 68 meters, or a total length overall length including the surrounding moat of about 800 meters The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the out of the mound. The burial facility is a horizontal entry stone chamber containing a stone sarcophagus. Despite having been looted, surviving grave goods include iron arrowheads, armor, Chinese scepters, horse fittings, glass ''[[magatama]]'', jasper tubular beads, and Sue pottery pottery. From these items, the tumulus estimated to have been built in the early to mid-6th century during the late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
=== Amazutsu Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Amazutsu Kofun'''|天堤古墳||}} was the fifth tumulus in the cluster. It was bulldozed in 1962 to create an orchard. Stone ornaments were recovered from the site.
<gallery> Monomiyagura Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Monomiyagura Kofun Nakanojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Naka-no-jō Kofun Hashinojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Hashi-no-jō Kofun </gallery>
The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of {{ill|Arisa Station|en|Arisa Station|WD=Q4790865|lt=Arisa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|WD=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173945 |title= 野津古墳群 |trans-title= Nozu kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> Furthermore, the [[Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]], the largest ''kofun'' in Kumamoto Prefecture is located on the same hill to the north.

It is composed of five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, of which four are currently extant. Several boat-shaped and box-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi}} have also been found in the surrounding area.Excavations have been carried out since 1993.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It has a length of 102 meterss, making it the largest in the group. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'', as well as stone ornaments. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 117 meters. The excavated items have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Hikawa Town, and some of these items are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. The tumulus was used as a {{ill|Air raid shelter|en|Air raid shelter|WD=Q389959|lt=Air raid shelter}} in World War II.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hashi-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hashi-no-jō Kofun'''|端ノ城古墳||}} is the southernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the last built. It has a length of 68 meters, or a total length overall length including the surrounding moat of about 800 meters The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the out of the mound. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nozu Kofun Group]][[en:Nozu Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nozu Kofun Group]][[en:Nozu Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nozu Kofun Group]][[en:Nozu Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野津古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55525131]] Wikidata name: Nozu Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nozu Kofun cluster | native_name = 野津古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Himenojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Hime-no-jō Kofun | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nozu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|33|37.77|N|130|41|35.41|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.56049167|N|130.69316667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hikawa, Kumamoto|en|Hikawa, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184800|lt=Hikawa, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|33|37.77|N|130|41|35.41|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Nozu Kofun'''|野津古墳群| Nozu Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Ryūhoku, Kumamoto|Ryūhoku]] neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Hikawa, Kumamoto|en|Hikawa, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184800|lt=Hikawa}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173945 |title= 野津古墳群 |trans-title= Nozu kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> Furthermore, the [[Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]], the largest ''kofun'' in Kumamoto Prefecture is located on the same hill to the north.
==Overview== The Nozu Kofun cluster is located on a hill at an elevation of 90 to 100 meters overlooking the plains of the Hikawa River basin and the {{ill|Yatsushiro Sea|en|Yatsushiro Sea|WD=Q49560|lt=Yatsushiro Sea}} on the western foot of the {{ill|Kyushu Mountains|en|Kyushu Mountains|WD=Q11370238|lt=Kyushu Mountains}} in central Kumamoto Prefecture. It is composed of five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, of which four are currently extant. Several boat-shaped and box-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi}} have also been found in the surrounding area.Excavations have been carried out since 1993.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
===Monomi Yagura Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Monomi Yagura Kofun'''|物見櫓古墳||}} is the westernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the first built. It has a length of 62 meters, making it also the smallest in the group. The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southwest. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but there is no trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', or of a surrounding moat. The multi-chamber horizontal-entry [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has a length of 11 meters. The Monomi-Yagura Tomb is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century. Despite having been looted in antiquity, surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include iron arrowheads and iron spears, fragments of armor, glass beads, earrings with pendants, as well as ceramic pottery and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. Among these items the gold earrings with pendants and the ceramic pottery are noteworthy as indications of exchanges with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}, and have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Hikawa Town.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hime-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hime-no-jō Kofun'''|姫ノ城古墳||}} is the northernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the second built. It has a length of 86 meters, and is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 115 meters. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa''. In addition, stone ornaments (two quivers, six lid caps, three lid supports, seven stone shields, etc.) have been found on the outside of the mound, which is the second largest concentration in any ''kofun'' after the [[Yame Kofun Cluster|Iwatoyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. These items have been designated Important Cultural Properties of Kumamoto Prefecture. Details of the burial chamber are not clear, as it has not been excavated, but {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar}} surveys indicate a horizontal-entry stone chamber. It is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Naka-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Naka-no-jō Kofun'''|中ノ城古墳||}} is the central tumulus in the group, and is believed to have been the third built. It has a length of 102 meterss, making it the largest in the group. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'', as well as stone ornaments. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 117 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, and was robbed in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. Surviving grave goods found include armor, iron swords, horse equipment, and glass beads, which date the construction to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. The excavated items have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Hikawa Town, and some of these items are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. The tumulus was used as a {{ill|Air raid shelter|en|Air raid shelter|WD=Q389959|lt=Air raid shelter}} in World War II.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hashi-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hashi-no-jō Kofun'''|端ノ城古墳||}} is the southernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the last built. It has a length of 68 meters, or a total length overall length including the surrounding moat of about 800 meters The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the out of the mound. The burial facility is a horizontal entry stone chamber containing a stone sarcophagus. Despite having been looted, surviving grave goods include iron arrowheads, armor, Chinese scepters, horse fittings, glass ''[[magatama]]'', jasper tubular beads, and Sue pottery pottery. From these items, the tumulus estimated to have been built in the early to mid-6th century during the late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
=== Amazutsu Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Amazutsu Kofun'''|天堤古墳||}} was the fifth tumulus in the cluster. It was bulldozed in 1962 to create an orchard. Stone ornaments were recovered from the site.
<gallery> Monomiyagura Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Monomiyagura Kofun Nakanojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Naka-no-jō Kofun Hashinojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Hashi-no-jō Kofun </gallery>
The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of {{ill|Arisa Station|en|Arisa Station|WD=Q4790865|lt=Arisa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|WD=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}}

It has a length of 62 meters, making it also the smallest in the group. The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southwest. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but there is no trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', or of a surrounding moat. The multi-chamber horizontal-entry [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has a length of 11 meters. The Monomi-Yagura Tomb is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century. Despite having been looted in antiquity, surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include iron arrowheads and iron spears, fragments of armor, glass beads, earrings with pendants, as well as ceramic pottery and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. Among these items the gold earrings with pendants and the ceramic pottery are noteworthy as indications of exchanges with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}, and have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Hikawa Town.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hime-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hime-no-jō Kofun'''|姫ノ城古墳||}} is the northernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the second built. It has a length of 86 meters, and is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 115 meters. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa''. In addition, stone ornaments (two quivers, six lid caps, three lid supports, seven stone shields, etc.) have been found on the outside of the mound, which is the second largest concentration in any ''kofun'' after the [[Yame Kofun Cluster|Iwatoyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. These items have been designated Important Cultural Properties of Kumamoto Prefecture. Details of the burial chamber are not clear, as it has not been excavated, but {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar}} surveys indicate a horizontal-entry stone chamber. It is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Naka-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Naka-no-jō Kofun'''|中ノ城古墳||}} is the central tumulus in the group, and is believed to have been the third built. It has a length of 102 meterss, making it the largest in the group. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'', as well as stone ornaments. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 117 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, and was robbed in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. Surviving grave goods found include armor, iron swords, horse equipment, and glass beads, which date the construction to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. The excavated items have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Hikawa Town, and some of these items are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. The tumulus was used as a {{ill|Air raid shelter|en|Air raid shelter|WD=Q389959|lt=Air raid shelter}} in World War II.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hashi-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hashi-no-jō Kofun'''|端ノ城古墳||}} is the southernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the last built. It has a length of 68 meters, or a total length overall length including the surrounding moat of about 800 meters The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the out of the mound. The burial facility is a horizontal entry stone chamber containing a stone sarcophagus. Despite having been looted, surviving grave goods include iron arrowheads, armor, Chinese scepters, horse fittings, glass ''[[magatama]]'', jasper tubular beads, and Sue pottery pottery. From these items, the tumulus estimated to have been built in the early to mid-6th century during the late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
=== Amazutsu Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Amazutsu Kofun'''|天堤古墳||}} was the fifth tumulus in the cluster. It was bulldozed in 1962 to create an orchard. Stone ornaments were recovered from the site.
<gallery> Monomiyagura Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Monomiyagura Kofun Nakanojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Naka-no-jō Kofun Hashinojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Hashi-no-jō Kofun </gallery>
The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of {{ill|Arisa Station|en|Arisa Station|WD=Q4790865|lt=Arisa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}}.

The {{nihongo|'''Nozu Kofun'''|野津古墳群| Nozu Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Ryūhoku, Kumamoto|Ryūhoku]] neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Hikawa, Kumamoto|en|Hikawa, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184800|lt=Hikawa}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173945 |title= 野津古墳群 |trans-title= Nozu kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> Furthermore, the [[Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]], the largest ''kofun'' in Kumamoto Prefecture is located on the same hill to the north.
==Overview== The Nozu Kofun cluster is located on a hill at an elevation of 90 to 100 meters overlooking the plains of the Hikawa River basin and the {{ill|Yatsushiro Sea|en|Yatsushiro Sea|WD=Q49560|lt=Yatsushiro Sea}} on the western foot of the {{ill|Kyushu Mountains|en|Kyushu Mountains|WD=Q11370238|lt=Kyushu Mountains}} in central Kumamoto Prefecture. It is composed of five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, of which four are currently extant. Several boat-shaped and box-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi}} have also been found in the surrounding area.Excavations have been carried out since 1993.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but there is no trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', or of a surrounding moat. The multi-chamber horizontal-entry [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has a length of 11 meters. The Monomi-Yagura Tomb is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century. Despite having been looted in antiquity, surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include iron arrowheads and iron spears, fragments of armor, glass beads, earrings with pendants, as well as ceramic pottery and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. Among these items the gold earrings with pendants and the ceramic pottery are noteworthy as indications of exchanges with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}, and have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Hikawa Town.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hime-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hime-no-jō Kofun'''|姫ノ城古墳||}} is the northernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the second built. These items have been designated Important Cultural Properties of Kumamoto Prefecture. Details of the burial chamber are not clear, as it has not been excavated, but {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar}} surveys indicate a horizontal-entry stone chamber. It is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Naka-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Naka-no-jō Kofun'''|中ノ城古墳||}} is the central tumulus in the group, and is believed to have been the third built. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 117 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, and was robbed in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. Surviving grave goods found include armor, iron swords, horse equipment, and glass beads, which date the construction to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. The excavated items have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Hikawa Town, and some of these items are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. The tumulus was used as a {{ill|Air raid shelter|en|Air raid shelter|WD=Q389959|lt=Air raid shelter}} in World War II.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hashi-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hashi-no-jō Kofun'''|端ノ城古墳||}} is the southernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the last built. It has a length of 68 meters, or a total length overall length including the surrounding moat of about 800 meters The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the out of the mound. From these items, the tumulus estimated to have been built in the early to mid-6th century during the late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of {{ill|Arisa Station|en|Arisa Station|WD=Q4790865|lt=Arisa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}}.

From these items, the tumulus estimated to have been built in the early to mid-6th century during the late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
=== Amazutsu Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Amazutsu Kofun'''|天堤古墳||}} was the fifth tumulus in the cluster. It was bulldozed in 1962 to create an orchard. Stone ornaments were recovered from the site.
<gallery> Monomiyagura Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Monomiyagura Kofun Nakanojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Naka-no-jō Kofun Hashinojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Hashi-no-jō Kofun </gallery>
The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of {{ill|Arisa Station|en|Arisa Station|WD=Q4790865|lt=Arisa Station|1=Arisa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nozu Kofun Group]][[en:Nozu Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nozu Kofun Group]][[en:Nozu Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Nozu Kofun Group]][[en:Nozu Kofun Cluster]][[ja:野津古墳群]]
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nozu Kofun cluster | native_name = 野津古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Himenojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Hime-no-jō Kofun | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nozu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|33|37.77|N|130|41|35.41|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.56049167|N|130.69316667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hikawa, Kumamoto|en|Hikawa, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184800|lt=Hikawa, Kumamoto|1=Hikawa, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|33|37.77|N|130|41|35.41|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Nozu Kofun'''|野津古墳群| Nozu Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Ryūhoku, Kumamoto|Ryūhoku]] neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Hikawa, Kumamoto|en|Hikawa, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184800|lt=Hikawa|1=Hikawa, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173945 |title= 野津古墳群 |trans-title= Nozu kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> Furthermore, the [[Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]], the largest ''kofun'' in Kumamoto Prefecture is located on the same hill to the north.
==Overview== The Nozu Kofun cluster is located on a hill at an elevation of 90 to 100 meters overlooking the plains of the Hikawa River basin and the {{ill|Yatsushiro Sea|en|Yatsushiro Sea|WD=Q49560|lt=Yatsushiro Sea|1=Yatsushiro Sea}} on the western foot of the {{ill|Kyushu Mountains|en|Kyushu Mountains|WD=Q11370238|lt=Kyushu Mountains|1=Kyushu Mountains}} in central Kumamoto Prefecture. It is composed of five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, of which four are currently extant. Several boat-shaped and box-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi|1=Sarcophagus}} have also been found in the surrounding area.Excavations have been carried out since 1993.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
===Monomi Yagura Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Monomi Yagura Kofun'''|物見櫓古墳||}} is the westernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the first built. It has a length of 62 meters, making it also the smallest in the group. The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southwest. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but there is no trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', or of a surrounding moat. The multi-chamber horizontal-entry [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has a length of 11 meters. The Monomi-Yagura Tomb is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century. Despite having been looted in antiquity, surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include iron arrowheads and iron spears, fragments of armor, glass beads, earrings with pendants, as well as ceramic pottery and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. Among these items the gold earrings with pendants and the ceramic pottery are noteworthy as indications of exchanges with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}, and have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Hikawa Town.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hime-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hime-no-jō Kofun'''|姫ノ城古墳||}} is the northernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the second built. It has a length of 86 meters, and is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 115 meters. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa''. In addition, stone ornaments (two quivers, six lid caps, three lid supports, seven stone shields, etc.) have been found on the outside of the mound, which is the second largest concentration in any ''kofun'' after the [[Yame Kofun Cluster|Iwatoyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. These items have been designated Important Cultural Properties of Kumamoto Prefecture. Details of the burial chamber are not clear, as it has not been excavated, but {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar|1=Ground-penetrating radar}} surveys indicate a horizontal-entry stone chamber. It is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Naka-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Naka-no-jō Kofun'''|中ノ城古墳||}} is the central tumulus in the group, and is believed to have been the third built. It has a length of 102 meterss, making it the largest in the group. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'', as well as stone ornaments. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 117 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, and was robbed in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. Surviving grave goods found include armor, iron swords, horse equipment, and glass beads, which date the construction to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. The excavated items have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Hikawa Town, and some of these items are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. The tumulus was used as a {{ill|Air raid shelter|en|Air raid shelter|WD=Q389959|lt=Air raid shelter|1=Air raid shelter}} in World War II.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hashi-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hashi-no-jō Kofun'''|端ノ城古墳||}} is the southernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the last built. It has a length of 68 meters, or a total length overall length including the surrounding moat of about 800 meters The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the out of the mound. The burial facility is a horizontal entry stone chamber containing a stone sarcophagus. Despite having been looted, surviving grave goods include iron arrowheads, armor, Chinese scepters, horse fittings, glass ''[[magatama]]'', jasper tubular beads, and Sue pottery pottery. From these items, the tumulus estimated to have been built in the early to mid-6th century during the late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
=== Amazutsu Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Amazutsu Kofun'''|天堤古墳||}} was the fifth tumulus in the cluster. It was bulldozed in 1962 to create an orchard. Stone ornaments were recovered from the site.
<gallery> Monomiyagura Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Monomiyagura Kofun Nakanojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Naka-no-jō Kofun Hashinojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Hashi-no-jō Kofun </gallery>
The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of {{ill|Arisa Station|en|Arisa Station|WD=Q4790865|lt=Arisa Station|1=Arisa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.

The {{nihongo|'''Nozu Kofun'''|野津古墳群| Nozu Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Ryūhoku, Kumamoto|Ryūhoku]] neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Hikawa, Kumamoto|en|Hikawa, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184800|lt=Hikawa|1=Hikawa, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173945 |title= 野津古墳群 |trans-title= Nozu kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> Furthermore, the [[Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]], the largest ''kofun'' in Kumamoto Prefecture is located on the same hill to the north.
==Overview== The Nozu Kofun cluster is located on a hill at an elevation of 90 to 100 meters overlooking the plains of the Hikawa River basin and the {{ill|Yatsushiro Sea|en|Yatsushiro Sea|WD=Q49560|lt=Yatsushiro Sea|1=Yatsushiro Sea}} on the western foot of the {{ill|Kyushu Mountains|en|Kyushu Mountains|WD=Q11370238|lt=Kyushu Mountains|1=Kyushu Mountains}} in central Kumamoto Prefecture. It is composed of five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, of which four are currently extant. Several boat-shaped and box-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi|1=Sarcophagus}} have also been found in the surrounding area.Excavations have been carried out since 1993.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
===Monomi Yagura Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Monomi Yagura Kofun'''|物見櫓古墳||}} is the westernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the first built. It has a length of 62 meters, making it also the smallest in the group. The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southwest. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but there is no trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', or of a surrounding moat. The multi-chamber horizontal-entry [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has a length of 11 meters. The Monomi-Yagura Tomb is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century. Despite having been looted in antiquity, surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include iron arrowheads and iron spears, fragments of armor, glass beads, earrings with pendants, as well as ceramic pottery and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. Among these items the gold earrings with pendants and the ceramic pottery are noteworthy as indications of exchanges with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}, and have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Hikawa Town.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hime-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hime-no-jō Kofun'''|姫ノ城古墳||}} is the northernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the second built. It has a length of 86 meters, and is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 115 meters. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa''. In addition, stone ornaments (two quivers, six lid caps, three lid supports, seven stone shields, etc.) have been found on the outside of the mound, which is the second largest concentration in any ''kofun'' after the [[Yame Kofun Cluster|Iwatoyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. These items have been designated Important Cultural Properties of Kumamoto Prefecture. Details of the burial chamber are not clear, as it has not been excavated, but {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar|1=Ground-penetrating radar}} surveys indicate a horizontal-entry stone chamber. It is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Naka-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Naka-no-jō Kofun'''|中ノ城古墳||}} is the central tumulus in the group, and is believed to have been the third built. It has a length of 102 meterss, making it the largest in the group. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'', as well as stone ornaments. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 117 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, and was robbed in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. Surviving grave goods found include armor, iron swords, horse equipment, and glass beads, which date the construction to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. The excavated items have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Hikawa Town, and some of these items are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. The tumulus was used as a {{ill|Air raid shelter|en|Air raid shelter|WD=Q389959|lt=Air raid shelter|1=Air raid shelter}} in World War II.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hashi-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hashi-no-jō Kofun'''|端ノ城古墳||}} is the southernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the last built. It has a length of 68 meters, or a total length overall length including the surrounding moat of about 800 meters The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the out of the mound. The burial facility is a horizontal entry stone chamber containing a stone sarcophagus. Despite having been looted, surviving grave goods include iron arrowheads, armor, Chinese scepters, horse fittings, glass ''[[magatama]]'', jasper tubular beads, and Sue pottery pottery. From these items, the tumulus estimated to have been built in the early to mid-6th century during the late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
=== Amazutsu Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Amazutsu Kofun'''|天堤古墳||}} was the fifth tumulus in the cluster. It was bulldozed in 1962 to create an orchard. Stone ornaments were recovered from the site.
<gallery> Monomiyagura Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Monomiyagura Kofun Nakanojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Naka-no-jō Kofun Hashinojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Hashi-no-jō Kofun </gallery>
The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of {{ill|Arisa Station|en|Arisa Station|WD=Q4790865|lt=Arisa Station|1=Arisa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|WD=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}}

==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Nozu Kofun Group}} *[https://www.town.hikawa.kumamoto.jp/kanko/kiji0033765/index.html Hikawa Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://kumamoto.guide/spots/detail/11431 Kumamoto Tourism Guide] {{in lang|ja}}

<gallery> Monomiyagura Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Monomiyagura Kofun Nakanojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Naka-no-jō Kofun Hashinojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Hashi-no-jō Kofun </gallery>

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173945 |title= 野津古墳群 |trans-title= Nozu kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> Furthermore, the [[Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]], the largest ''kofun'' in Kumamoto Prefecture is located on the same hill to the north.
==Overview== The Nozu Kofun cluster is located on a hill at an elevation of 90 to 100 meters overlooking the plains of the Hikawa River basin and the {{ill|Yatsushiro Sea|en|Yatsushiro Sea|WD=Q49560|lt=Yatsushiro Sea|1=Yatsushiro Sea}} on the western foot of the {{ill|Kyushu Mountains|en|Kyushu Mountains|WD=Q11370238|lt=Kyushu Mountains|1=Kyushu Mountains}} in central Kumamoto Prefecture. It is composed of five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, of which four are currently extant. Several boat-shaped and box-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi|1=Sarcophagus}} have also been found in the surrounding area.Excavations have been carried out since 1993.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
===Monomi Yagura Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Monomi Yagura Kofun'''|物見櫓古墳||}} is the westernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the first built. It has a length of 62 meters, making it also the smallest in the group. The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southwest. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but there is no trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', or of a surrounding moat. The multi-chamber horizontal-entry [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has a length of 11 meters. The Monomi-Yagura Tomb is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century. Despite having been looted in antiquity, surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include iron arrowheads and iron spears, fragments of armor, glass beads, earrings with pendants, as well as ceramic pottery and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. Among these items the gold earrings with pendants and the ceramic pottery are noteworthy as indications of exchanges with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}, and have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Hikawa Town.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hime-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hime-no-jō Kofun'''|姫ノ城古墳||}} is the northernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the second built. It has a length of 86 meters, and is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 115 meters. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa''. In addition, stone ornaments (two quivers, six lid caps, three lid supports, seven stone shields, etc.) have been found on the outside of the mound, which is the second largest concentration in any ''kofun'' after the [[Yame Kofun Cluster|Iwatoyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. These items have been designated Important Cultural Properties of Kumamoto Prefecture. Details of the burial chamber are not clear, as it has not been excavated, but {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar|1=Ground-penetrating radar}} surveys indicate a horizontal-entry stone chamber. It is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Naka-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Naka-no-jō Kofun'''|中ノ城古墳||}} is the central tumulus in the group, and is believed to have been the third built. It has a length of 102 meterss, making it the largest in the group. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'', as well as stone ornaments. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 117 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, and was robbed in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. Surviving grave goods found include armor, iron swords, horse equipment, and glass beads, which date the construction to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. The excavated items have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Hikawa Town, and some of these items are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. The tumulus was used as a {{ill|Air raid shelter|en|Air raid shelter|WD=Q389959|lt=Air raid shelter|1=Air raid shelter}} in World War II.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hashi-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hashi-no-jō Kofun'''|端ノ城古墳||}} is the southernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the last built. It has a length of 68 meters, or a total length overall length including the surrounding moat of about 800 meters The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the out of the mound. The burial facility is a horizontal entry stone chamber containing a stone sarcophagus. Despite having been looted, surviving grave goods include iron arrowheads, armor, Chinese scepters, horse fittings, glass ''[[magatama]]'', jasper tubular beads, and Sue pottery pottery. From these items, the tumulus estimated to have been built in the early to mid-6th century during the late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
=== Amazutsu Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Amazutsu Kofun'''|天堤古墳||}} was the fifth tumulus in the cluster. It was bulldozed in 1962 to create an orchard. Stone ornaments were recovered from the site.
<gallery> Monomiyagura Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Monomiyagura Kofun Nakanojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Naka-no-jō Kofun Hashinojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Hashi-no-jō Kofun </gallery>
The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of {{ill|Arisa Station|en|Arisa Station|WD=Q4790865|lt=Arisa Station|1=Arisa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.

The {{nihongo|'''Nozu Kofun'''|野津古墳群| Nozu Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Ryūhoku, Kumamoto|Ryūhoku]] neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Hikawa, Kumamoto|en|Hikawa, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184800|lt=Hikawa|1=Hikawa, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173945 |title= 野津古墳群 |trans-title= Nozu kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> Furthermore, the [[Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]], the largest ''kofun'' in Kumamoto Prefecture is located on the same hill to the north.

Several boat-shaped and box-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi|1=Sarcophagus}} have also been found in the surrounding area.Excavations have been carried out since 1993.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

Among these items the gold earrings with pendants and the ceramic pottery are noteworthy as indications of exchanges with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}, and have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Hikawa Town.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 117 meters. These items have been designated Important Cultural Properties of Kumamoto Prefecture. Details of the burial chamber are not clear, as it has not been excavated, but {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar|1=Ground-penetrating radar}} surveys indicate a horizontal-entry stone chamber. It is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Naka-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Naka-no-jō Kofun'''|中ノ城古墳||}} is the central tumulus in the group, and is believed to have been the third built. It has a length of 102 meterss, making it the largest in the group. The mound is orientated to the south-southwest and has both ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'', as well as stone ornaments. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat, giving it a total overall length of 117 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, and was robbed in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. Surviving grave goods found include armor, iron swords, horse equipment, and glass beads, which date the construction to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. The excavated items have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Hikawa Town, and some of these items are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. The tumulus was used as a {{ill|Air raid shelter|en|Air raid shelter|WD=Q389959|lt=Air raid shelter|1=Air raid shelter}} in World War II.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hashi-no-jō Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Hashi-no-jō Kofun'''|端ノ城古墳||}} is the southernmost in the group, and is believed to have been the last built. It has a length of 68 meters, or a total length overall length including the surrounding moat of about 800 meters The mound is constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the out of the mound. The burial facility is a horizontal entry stone chamber containing a stone sarcophagus. Despite having been looted, surviving grave goods include iron arrowheads, armor, Chinese scepters, horse fittings, glass ''[[magatama]]'', jasper tubular beads, and Sue pottery pottery. From these items, the tumulus estimated to have been built in the early to mid-6th century during the late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
=== Amazutsu Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Amazutsu Kofun'''|天堤古墳||}} was the fifth tumulus in the cluster. It was bulldozed in 1962 to create an orchard. Stone ornaments were recovered from the site.
<gallery> Monomiyagura Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Monomiyagura Kofun Nakanojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Naka-no-jō Kofun Hashinojo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Hashi-no-jō Kofun </gallery>
The site is about 4.3 kilometers east of {{ill|Arisa Station|en|Arisa Station|WD=Q4790865|lt=Arisa Station|1=Arisa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.
